specification.topic-statement: The agent reap system removes dead agents from memory and marks them as reaped in the database, freeing their resources while preserving their audit trail.

scope.in-scope: Bulk and targeted reap modes, descendant collection, database status updates, in-memory removal, current-agent switching when the active agent or its ancestor is reaped, and result reporting to the scrollback.
scope.boundary: How an agent is killed and its status set to dead is handled by the agent termination subsystem. Reap operates only on agents that are already dead.
Marking agents as reaped in the database is delegated to the database layer. The reap operation calls into that layer but does not implement the underlying SQL.

field.name: target identifier
field.note: optional agent UUID or unique prefix; absent triggers bulk mode

behavior.mode-selection:
The reap command operates in one of two modes based on whether a target identifier is supplied.
When no argument is given, bulk mode is used: all dead agents currently loaded in the REPL are collected as victims.
When an argument is given, targeted mode is used: the argument is matched against the in-memory agent list as a UUID or unique prefix. If no match is found, a warning is appended to the scrollback and the command completes without reaping. If the prefix matches more than one agent, a warning about ambiguity is appended and the command completes without reaping. If the matched agent is not dead, a warning is appended and the command completes without reaping.
behavior.bulk-collection: In bulk mode, all agents in the REPL's in-memory agent list whose dead flag is set are collected as victims. If no dead agents are found, a message is appended to the scrollback and the command completes without reaping.
behavior.targeted-collection:
In targeted mode, the matched agent and all of its descendants are collected as victims, regardless of whether the descendants are dead or alive. Descendants are collected by depth-first traversal through the in-memory agent list. The collection is bounded at 256 total agents (target plus descendants).
Targeted reap collects both dead and alive descendants of the target. This prevents orphaned subtrees from remaining in memory after their parent is reaped.
behavior.current-agent-safety-check:
Before removing any agent, the system checks whether the current active agent or any of its ancestors appears in the victim list. If so, the current agent must be switched before reaping begins.
A replacement agent is found by scanning the in-memory agent list for the first agent that is not in the victim list. If no such agent exists, reaping is aborted: a warning is appended to the scrollback and the command returns without making any changes.
The active agent is redirected to a surviving agent before any victim is freed. This prevents the REPL from holding a reference to a deallocated agent context.
behavior.removal:
Each victim is processed in the collected order.
For each victim, the operation checks whether the agent is still present in the in-memory list (a victim may have been removed when its parent was reaped earlier in the same pass). If still present, the agent is removed from the in-memory list and its status is set to reaped in the database. The agent's memory allocation is then freed.
Database rows are not deleted. Marking an agent as reaped preserves the audit trail while signaling that the agent's in-memory resources have been released.
behavior.result-reporting: After all victims are processed, a message is appended to the current agent's scrollback reporting the total number of agents reaped.
behavior.error-behavior: If a database operation fails during removal, the error is returned and no further victims are processed. In-memory removal of already-processed victims is not undone.
